notes_from_pm: formerly XII
doorways in outer part usurped from Ramesses V.
Inaccessible after Corridor B.
Temp. Merneptaḥ-Siptaḥ.
wife of Sethos II. Usurped by Setnakht.
formerly XI
son of Ramesses IX.
Excavated by Davis, and by Carnarvon and Carter.
Excavated by Belzoni.
1st ed. 24
Standard-bearer, Child of the nursery. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. Excavated by Loret.
Excavated by Loret.

Uninscribed tomb, attributed to Amenophis I by Weigall in Ann. Serv. xi (1911), pp. 174-5 [12], and id. A Guide to the Antiquities of Upper Egypt, pp. 163-4, but this is not supported by any inscriptional evidence, and does not correspond with the position given in the Abbott Papyrus (cf. Peet, The Great Tomb-Robberies of the Twentieth Egyptian Dynasty, pp. 37-8). See also the tomb of Queen ʿAhmosi Nefertere, infra, p. 599.
(?). Excavated by Loret
Excavated by Davis.
Overseer of the Fields of Amūn, Dyn. XVIII, re-used by Merenkhons, Doorkeeper of the House of Amūn, Dyn. XXII (name from scarab). Excavated by Davis and Carter.
Divine father, and Chief of the harîm of Amūn, parents of Queen Teye.
Governor of the town, Vizier. Temp. Amenophis II. (Also owner of Theb. tb. 29.)
Probably Amenophis IV, formerly attributed to Queen Teye or to Smenkhkarēʿ.
'Gold Tomb', uninscribed.
1st ed. 58. Excavated by Carnarvon and Carter.
Dyn. XX(?). Buried. (L. D. Text, No. 14, Bibl. i, 1st ed. No. 37.)
A PRINCESS, no name.
wife of Ramesses I. Unfinished. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 7, L. D. Text, No. 13, WILKINSON, No. 19.)
A QUEEN, cartouche blank. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 6, L. D. Text, No. 12, WILKINSON, No. 18.)
Charioteer of the stable of the Great House, son of Ramesses III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8, L. D. Text, No. 11, WILKINSON, No. 16, HAY, No. 1.)
King's son, Hereditary prince of the royal children of his Majesty, Charioteer of the Great Stable, &c., son of Ramesses III.
Sem-priest of Ptaḥ, son of Ramesses III.
(probably). Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis I. (Bibl. i, 1st ed. p. 49.)
daughter of Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa and Sit-ḏḥout. Dyn. XVII. (Bibl. i, 1st ed. p. 49.)
mother of Ramesses VI, daughter of Ḥubalzanet (i.e. Ḥemzert). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 4, L. D. Text, No. 10, WILKINSON, No. 14.)
Ramesside. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 3, L. D. Text, No. 9, WILKINSON, No. 12, HAY, No. 2.)
son of Ramesses III. (L. D. Text, No. 8, WILKINSON, No. 11, HAY, No. 3.)
Royal scribe, Overseer of horses, son of Ramesses III.
(?). daughter of Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 11, L. D. Text, No. 6, HAY, 2nd tomb east.)
wife of Ramesses II.
daughter of Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 12, L. D. Text, No. 5, HAY, No. 4.)
daughter of Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 13, L. D. Text, No. 4, HAY, No. 5.)
A PRINCESS, no name. Dyn. XX. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 14, L. D. Text, No. 3, HAY, No. 6.)
Great King's mother and King's wife. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 2, HAY, No. 7.)
A QUEEN, no name. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 16, L. D. Text, No. 1, HAY, No. 8.)
Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. Father, Khaʿbekhnet (name on fragment, BRUYÈRE, Rapport (1927), fig. 34 [4]). Wife, Iyneferti.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 107.) Parents, Sennezem (tomb 1) and Iyneferti. Wives, Saḥte and (probably) Esi.
Servant in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes. Ramesside. Parents, Menna and Huy. Wife, Nezemtbehdet.
Chiseller of Amūn in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 106.) Parents, Thonūfer, Chiseller of Amūn in the Khenu, and Maʿetnefert. Wives, Nefertere and Ḥenutmehyt.
Servant in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes. Ramesside. Parents, Neferronpet and Mahi (name on stela in Brit. Mus. 150, see infra, p. 14). Wife, Taēsi.
Foremen in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ḥaremḥab to Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 101.) Wife (of Neferḥōtep), Iymau; (of Nebnūfer), Iy.
Scribe in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 99.) Parents, Amenemḥab and Kakaia. Wife, Mutemwia.
Chief in the Great Place. Temp. Amenophis II, Tuthmosis IV, and Amenophis III. (L. D. Text, No. 96.) Wife, Meryt.
Servant in the Place of Truth, Charmer of scorpions. Ramesside. Wife, Tent-hōm.

Servants in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 97.) Father (of Penbuy), Iri (name from offering-table of Penbuy, in Turin Mus. 1559). Wives (of Penbuy), Amentetusert and Irnūfer; (of Kasa), Bukhaʿnef.
See also Tomb 20, supra, p. 546. Sarcophagus as Queen-Consort, quartzite, in Cairo Mus. Ent. 47032.
Probably Princess Neferureʿ, daughter of Ḥatshepsut.
Temp. Tuthmosis III.
Wife of King Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa. Found by Mariette in 1859.
Daughter of ʿAḥḥotp (wife of King Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa).
Tomb of Queen ʿAḥmosi Nefertere (probably). Attributed to Amenophis I by Carter, later equated by Černý with 'House of Amenophis of the Garden'.
Eldest son of King Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa and ʿAḥḥotp.
Found by Mariette in 1860. Pyramid, see Hay MSS. 29816.
Probably younger brother and successor of Antef (Sekhemreʿ-Wepmaʿet).
Royal acquaintance. Dyn. XVII. Objects found by Vassalli in 1863.
Found by Mariette in 1857. For pyramid, possibly of Kamosi, see infra, p. 620.
Wife of King Ḏḥuti. Found in tomb by Passalacqua.
Unfinished.
Scribe of the Great Harîm, probably temp. Antef (Nubkheperrēʿ). Rock-tomb, uninscribed. Found by Mariette in 1860, probably near Theb. tb. 13.
Pyramid behind Theb. tb. 24, perhaps belonging to this tomb.
